audit_items.proof_trace: write_section_payload accesses sec->binary->data, where sec->binary is a raw back-pointer set to the owning LinkerInputBinary at parse time (binary-reader-linker.cc:184). All LinkerInputBinary objects are owned by ctx->inputs (vector<unique_ptr<LinkerInputBinary>>) in the stack-allocated Context, and ctx->inputs is never modified during perform_link/write_binary, so no binary is freed before write_section_payload executes. The ~LinkerInputBinary destructor (which calls delete[] data) only runs when ProgramMain's stack frame unwinds after perform_link has returned, guaranteeing no UAF or double-free.
